title: Validate input for the --platform parameter
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

There is no validation on the syntax of the --platform parameter to build

Like when using the multi-arch syntax from `buildx build`, by mistake.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman build --platform=linux/amd64,linux/arm64 multiarch

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

podman build --platform=linux/amd64,linux/arm64 multiarch

```
       "Architecture": "amd64,linux",
        "Os": "linux",
```

Running with podman4 gives a different error:

`Error: error creating build container: choosing an image from manifest list docker://busybox:latest: no image found in manifest list for architecture amd64,linux, variant "arm64", OS linux`

**Describe the results you expected:**

docker build --platform=linux/amd64,linux/arm64 multiarch

`Error response from daemon: "amd64,linux" is an invalid component of "linux/amd64,linux/arm64": platform specifier component must match "^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$": invalid argument`


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.2.3
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/unknown,now 100:3.2.3-1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Ubuntu 20.04

comments.body: `--platform` doesn't currently take a list, so it's probably parsing "linux/amd64,linux/arm64" as os="linux", arch="amd64,linux", variant="arm64".  Like @rhatdan mentioned, multiple `--platform` values is something buildah's development branch just started recognizing, and podman 3.2.3 used a recent release version that wouldn't have that feature.

It is indeed parsing it as you say. If comma is a valid character in a platform, I guess there would be a problem...
I was just wondering if you should make it fail (with the comma), until it can parse the value as an array instead ?
Since the upstream handle's the "," I think we are ok in the main branch correct?
It landed on the buildah "main" branch after it branched for v1.22, so the podman "main" branch, which is currently using buildah v1.22.0, doesn't include it yet.

If multi-arch will be handled properly (through buildah) in podman4, then it is only a (parsing) problem for podman3 EDIT: Confirmed, it's working OK with `buildah bud`. Only remaining feature is to create a multi-arch manifest (for both). 
$ ./bin/buildah --version

4ebdd8a3dffaebee97c0336878b1bb00ead3968585a1f2d665df236c234454ae
You could consider adding validation to the branches for podman2 (1.18) and podman3 (1.21), or simply ignore it altogether.

> If multi-arch will be handled properly (through buildah) in podman4, then it is only a (parsing) problem for podman3
> 
> EDIT: Confirmed, it's working OK with `buildah bud`. Only remaining feature is to create a multi-arch manifest (for both).

Using the `--manifest` flag instead of the `-t` flag should take care of this.  `-t` can commit directly to a registry or other non-`containers-storage:` location, while the logic behind `--manifest` can't, so the CLI front end doesn't automatically try to substitute one for the other.
Thanks, that makes sense (and Docker has the same limitation of course, have to use BuildKit and Registry)

I believe we can close this issue, since it is fixed in Main.  If you want to have validation back ported to older versions then open a PR.  Personally I would just move forward, we can push out a new version of Buildah soon with the new feature.
